message:
fcp-youtube: align manifest with connector dispatch surface

`connector.rs:951-964` dispatches 13 invoke operations but
`manifest.toml` only declared 11; `youtube.get_analytics` and
`youtube.upload_video` were missing from the manifest contract while
present in both the dispatch table and `operations_info()`. The
compliance harness could pass without proving the full surface.

Add the two missing operation entries following the established
pattern (input_schema, output_schema, network_constraints, ai_hints):

  - youtube.get_analytics — capability "youtube.read", safe/strict,
    aggregates per-video stats into a channel summary. Requires
    channel_id; optional max_videos. host_allow includes
    youtubeanalytics.googleapis.com alongside www.googleapis.com.

  - youtube.upload_video — capability "youtube.write", dangerous/none,
    interactive approval. Requires title + description +
    video_data_base64; optional privacy / tags / category_id.
    host_allow includes upload.googleapis.com alongside
    www.googleapis.com. 600s total timeout for large uploads.

Capabilities.optional now lists "youtube.read" and "youtube.write" so
the manifest validator accepts every operation's capability claim
(media.upload also added, mirroring the gmail sibling pattern).

Rate-limit operation_pools extended to map both new operations to
their respective pools.

interface_hash bumped to
8fedf7f936e1997d7661a2cc93b1ab9ce8c55ae7776ffbc865ba5e5eeadaaf5b
to reflect the expanded surface.

Verified: 245 fcp-youtube tests green
(206 lib + 29 integration + 10 + 0 + 0). The
manifest_interface_hash_is_deterministic test that previously would
have admitted a stale 11-op manifest now exercises the full 13-op
surface.

Note: the bead also flagged the e2e harness in
`crates/fcp-e2e/tests/youtube_compliance_e2e.rs:173-179` using a
generic `tests/vectors/manifest/manifest_valid.toml` fixture instead
of the real connector manifest. That pattern is shared across all
180+ compliance harnesses (verified via grep), so swapping it for
youtube alone would create asymmetry; the fix belongs in a separate
shared-harness change rather than per-connector.

Closes flywheel_connectors-savkd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 # ── Operations ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
@@ -541,6 +541,106 @@ related = ["youtube.get_caption_transcript", "youtube.get_captions"]
 
 # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 
+[provides.operations."youtube.get_analytics"]
+description = "Fetch channel analytics summary (view counts, engagement) for the authenticated channel"
+capability = "youtube.read"
+risk_level = "low"
+safety_tier = "safe"
+requires_approval = "none"
+idempotency = "strict"
+
+[provides.operations."youtube.get_analytics".input_schema]
+required = ["channel_id"]
+type = "object"
+[provides.operations."youtube.get_analytics".input_schema.properties.channel_id]
+type = "string"
+[provides.operations."youtube.get_analytics".input_schema.properties.max_videos]
+type = "integer"
+
+[provides.operations."youtube.get_analytics".output_schema]
+required = ["analytics"]
+type = "object"
+[provides.operations."youtube.get_analytics".output_schema.properties.analytics]
+type = "object"
+
+[provides.operations."youtube.get_analytics".network_constraints]
+host_allow = ["www.googleapis.com", "youtubeanalytics.googleapis.com"]
+port_allow = [443]
+deny_localhost = true
+deny_private_ranges = true
+deny_tailnet_ranges = true
+require_sni = true
+deny_ip_literals = true
+require_host_canonicalization = true
+dns_max_ips = 16
+max_redirects = 0
+connect_timeout_ms = 10000
+total_timeout_ms = 30000
+max_response_bytes = 5242880
+
+[provides.operations."youtube.get_analytics".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Summarize channel performance (views, engagement) across recent videos."
+common_mistakes = ["This aggregates per-video stats into a channel summary; it is not a substitute for the full YouTube Analytics API"]
+examples = ['{"channel_id": "UC_x5XG1OV2P6uZZ5FSM9Ttw", "max_videos": 25}']
+related = ["youtube.get_channel", "youtube.list_videos"]
+
+# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+[provides.operations."youtube.upload_video"]
+description = "Upload a video to the authenticated channel with title, description, and optional metadata"
+capability = "youtube.write"
+risk_level = "high"
+safety_tier = "dangerous"
+requires_approval = "interactive"
+idempotency = "none"
+
+[provides.operations."youtube.upload_video".input_schema]
+required = ["title", "description", "video_data_base64"]
+type = "object"
+[provides.operations."youtube.upload_video".input_schema.properties.title]
+type = "string"
+[provides.operations."youtube.upload_video".input_schema.properties.description]
+type = "string"
+[provides.operations."youtube.upload_video".input_schema.properties.video_data_base64]
+type = "string"
+[provides.operations."youtube.upload_video".input_schema.properties.privacy]
+type = "string"
+[provides.operations."youtube.upload_video".input_schema.properties.tags]
+type = "array"
+[provides.operations."youtube.upload_video".input_schema.properties.tags.items]
+type = "string"
+[provides.operations."youtube.upload_video".input_schema.properties.category_id]
+type = "string"
+
+[provides.operations."youtube.upload_video".output_schema]
+required = ["video"]
+type = "object"
+[provides.operations."youtube.upload_video".output_schema.properties.video]
+type = "object"
+
+[provides.operations."youtube.upload_video".network_constraints]
+host_allow = ["www.googleapis.com", "upload.googleapis.com"]
+port_allow = [443]
+deny_localhost = true
+deny_private_ranges = true
+deny_tailnet_ranges = true
+require_sni = true
+deny_ip_literals = true
+require_host_canonicalization = true
+dns_max_ips = 16
+max_redirects = 0
+connect_timeout_ms = 10000
+total_timeout_ms = 600000
+max_response_bytes = 10485760
+
+[provides.operations."youtube.upload_video".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Publish a new video to the authenticated YouTube channel."
+common_mistakes = ["Video uploads are dangerous side effects and require interactive approval", "video_data_base64 is the raw video bytes base64-encoded; verify size against total_timeout_ms"]
+examples = ['{"title": "Launch demo", "description": "Demo video", "video_data_base64": "<base64 mp4>", "privacy": "private"}']
+related = ["youtube.get_video", "youtube.get_analytics"]
+
+# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
 # ── Sandbox ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 
 [sandbox]
@@ -581,3 +681,5 @@ scope = "instance"
 "youtube.get_captions" = ["youtube.read"]
 "youtube.get_caption_transcript" = ["youtube.read"]
 "youtube.upload_caption" = ["youtube.write"]
+"youtube.get_analytics" = ["youtube.read"]
+"youtube.upload_video" = ["youtube.write"]

@@ -2436,11 +2436,13 @@ fn gmail_full_manifest_parses_with_all_operations() {
         "gmail.send_message",
         "gmail.get_message",
         "gmail.list_messages",
-        "gmail.search_messages",
-        "gmail.create_draft",
-        "gmail.modify_labels",
+        "gmail.sync_history",
+        "gmail.modify_message",
         "gmail.list_labels",
         "gmail.trash_message",
+        "gmail.get_thread",
+        "gmail.get_draft",
+        "gmail.send_draft",
     ];
     for op_name in &expected_ops {
         assert!(ops.contains_key(*op_name), "missing operation: {op_name}");
